title: Spatio-temporal transcriptomic analysis reveals distinct nephrotoxicity, DNA damage, and regeneration response after cisplatin
claims: Cisplatin induces early DNA damage in cortical and outer-medulla proximal tubules but not glomeruli.
Outer-medulla proximal tubules show sustained DNA damage response, inflammation, actin cytoskeletal remodeling, increased glycolytic metabolism, and suppression of mitochondrial activity.
Later responses include regeneration-related cell cycle pathway activation and ribosomal biogenesis in injured outer-medulla proximal tubules.
Whole kidney transcriptome analysis is less sensitive than OMPT for detecting nephrotoxicity mechanisms.

title: N-Acetylcysteine Prevents Skeletal Muscle Cisplatin-Induced Atrophy by Inducing Myogenic microRNAs and Maintaining the Redox Balance
claims: N-acetylcysteine (NAC) prevents skeletal muscle atrophy induced by cisplatin through redox regulation and modulation of myogenic microRNAs.
